compiled_state.title: Hook error messages display unparsed path variables like ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Error messages from hook dispatchers (e.g. pre-bash.sh) display raw ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} instead of the resolved path. Example: 'Error: PreToolUse:Bash hook error: [${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/dispatchers/pre-bash.sh]: BLOCKED'. The variable should be expanded before being included in user-visible output.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Test: verifying outbound bridge trigger after workflow fix
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: mechanical. hooks.json uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} twice per entry — Claude Code displays raw template variable in error messages. Fix: pass relative dispatcher names, resolve in run-hook.sh.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed in commit 2f2d7ce9: plugin.json uses relative dispatcher paths, run-hook.sh resolves them against hooks/ dir. Error messages now show one ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} instead of two.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
